what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that registers an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Fields: id att-685db34b, channel design-assets, attachment_type images, target image://design-assets/icons/Files, added ISO timestamp
The target uses an image:// URI scheme
It is an attachment record rather than a message or code file
standouts: added timestamp is precise to microseconds with timezone
conventions: Asset target expressed as a URI
concerns: No information about image format, dimensions, or alt text
No explicit version or checksum for the referenced asset
The image:// URI scheme is not further described or validated in this file
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links the design-assets channel to an image asset at icons/Files.
questions: What image format and resolution does the target asset have?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is this asset referenced by any channel messages or design documents?
What does the image:// URI scheme resolve to in storage?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record an image attachment for the design-assets channel, and the file does exactly that by storing the attachment type and target URI.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a discoverable, timestamped ledger entry that binds a channel to a specific image asset so the asset can be retrieved and referenced within the channel context.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme as the attachment target is a distinct convention compared to typical file paths or URLs.
contribution: It contributes a structured, timestamped record that maps a channel to a specific image asset, supporting asset provenance, retrieval, and channel-based organization in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel attachment metadata record for the design-assets channel, registering an image attachment of type images that targets image://design-assets/icons/Files and includes an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog an image asset in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced and retrieved as a channel attachment.
